items.briefing:
Scammers and cybercriminals continue to find creative ways to trick everyday users. Shared ChatGPT links are currently being abused to host fake "service outage" pages that trick people into downloading malware — so be cautious if you receive a link claiming a popular service is down. Separately, a new analysis highlights that organisations are increasingly slow to disclose data breaches, meaning your personal information may be exposed for months before you're notified.

For staff at this RTO, treat unexpected links — even those appearing to come from familiar platforms — with suspicion. If something seems off, report it to your IT contact before clicking.

featured.title: DevOps platform vulnerabilities rise in 2025 report
featured.quote: DevOps vulnerabilities threaten Australian software development infrastructure and require urgent patching.
